items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm so tired of my playlist. Every song sounds like the same three pop singers.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went through that last semester. What kinds of things have you actually liked recently?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I guess songs with strong storytelling? Like a clear narrator and a few real-life details.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then you should try indie folk. There's a singer named Hana Bryant — she basically writes short stories. Her album from last fall, the one called 'Bus Stop Mornings,' is the place to start.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How does it compare to the regular pop stuff?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The arrangements are sparser — usually just guitar, sometimes piano. The whole point is that you focus on the lyrics.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds like exactly what I need. I keep skipping tracks because they all blur together.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then I'd say start with the third track, 'Letters to Iowa.' If that one doesn't pull you in, you can give the rest a miss.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, third track. I'll listen on the bus tonight.

questions.stem: According to the man, what makes Hana Bryant's music different from the pop songs the woman is tired of?

options.A: The arrangements are sparser and meant to focus attention on the lyrics
options.B: The songs are produced entirely with electronic instruments
options.C: The albums are usually shorter than ten minutes
options.D: The singer rarely writes her own material

questions.explanation: 男生明说编曲简洁，多为吉他和钢琴，重点是让你专注歌词。A 正确。B 与原文相反，reversed_situation。C 没有依据，over_inference。D 与男生称她“写短篇小说”相反，wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy the entire album on physical CD this evening
options.B: Send Hana Bryant a personal email asking for new music
options.C: Listen to the third track from 'Bus Stop Mornings' on the bus tonight
options.D: Cancel her current pop music subscription completely

questions.explanation: 女生最后明确说今晚在公车上从第三首听起。C 正确。A 没提到买实体专辑，over_inference。B 完全没有这一情节，unrelated_next_step。D 是过度推断，over_inference。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You saw 'Lighthouse Echo' last night, right? What did you think?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm torn. The plot twists were really clever — I didn't see the ending coming at all.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's what everyone keeps saying online.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: But it was way too long. Almost three hours, and at least forty minutes in the middle could have been cut.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Like a slow stretch in the second act?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. There's this whole subplot about the brother that doesn't go anywhere. By the time the twist arrives, you've already lost some momentum.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did the audience seem bored?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A few people behind me actually got up. Not walked out, but went to get popcorn around the ninety-minute mark.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. I was thinking of seeing it tomorrow. Worth it for the twists?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: If you go in expecting a long ride, yes. Don't watch it tired.

questions.stem: What is the man's main complaint about the film?

options.A: The acting performances were uneven across the cast
options.B: The film was nearly three hours long, with a slow middle act that should have been cut
options.C: The opening twist was given away too early in the trailer
options.D: The soundtrack distracted from the film's most important scenes

questions.explanation: 男生明说时长接近三小时，中间部分本可以削减四十分钟。B 正确。A、C、D 男生都未提及，分别是 wrong_topic、over_inference、wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What does the man imply when he says "Don't watch it tired"?

options.A: The film should only be watched in a movie theater, not at home
options.B: The woman should plan to see the film with a large group of friends
options.C: The film contains scenes that are unsuitable for evening viewing
options.D: The film's length and slow middle will be harder to enjoy if she is already tired

questions.explanation: 男生暗示电影偏长、中段慢，疲倦时更难坚持。D 正确。A 没提观影方式，over_inference。B 没提到一群朋友，unrelated_next_step。C 是过度联想，over_inference。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm looking at the train schedule for Saturday — for our trip to Westport.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Anything reasonable in the morning?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: There's a 7:15, an 8:40, and a 10:05. The 7:15 is express, only an hour. The others stop everywhere and take almost two.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Seven-fifteen is brutal on a weekend. How's the price?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Express is $24 one-way. Local trains are $14.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Ten bucks each way isn't bad. What if we take the 8:40 out, spend the day, and grab a fast one back?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The last express back is 7:30 p.m. Local at 8:15 if we miss it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Eight-forty out, seven-thirty back, makes about nine hours there. That feels right. Booking now?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, let me just check that there are still two seats on the 8:40.

questions.stem: How much does the express train cost for one direction?
questions.explanation: 女生明确说快车单程 $24。C 正确。A、B、D 都是数字陷阱：$10 是两种车价差，$14 是慢车价，$30 没出现。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Check whether two seats are still available on the 8:40 train
options.B: Call Westport to confirm a hotel reservation for Saturday
options.C: Look up the local bus schedule from the train station to downtown
options.D: Cancel the trip and rebook for the following weekend

questions.explanation: 女生在最后明说要先确认 8:40 还有两个座位。A 正确。B、C 都是未出现的下一步，unrelated_next_step。D 与达成共识相反，reversed_situation。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you finish the watercolor class at the community center?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Last week, actually. Six sessions, every Tuesday evening.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Was it worth signing up?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, more than I expected. The instructor focused on three basic skills: mixing colors, controlling water, and layering washes. By session four we were doing small landscapes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you need to bring your own materials?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The first session everything was provided — paper, brushes, paints. From session two on, you had to bring your own paper. Brushes and paints stayed in the room.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was worried about the cost of supplies. How much did the paper come to?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I spent about eighteen dollars at the art store on a pad that lasted the whole course.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's manageable. I think I'll sign up for the next round.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They open registration in two weeks. Don't wait — the evening section fills up fast.

questions.stem: What did students need to bring to the watercolor class starting from the second session?

options.A: A set of brushes and an apron
options.B: A small framed canvas for landscape work
options.C: Their own selection of dry paint pigments
options.D: Their own paper for the class exercises

questions.explanation: 男生说从第二节起需要自带纸张，画笔和颜料留在教室。D 正确。A、C 与原文相反，reversed_situation 或 wrong_detail。B 没有此环节，wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do?

options.A: Avoid the watercolor class because it requires expensive materials
options.B: Register early when the next round opens because evening sections fill quickly
options.C: Choose a daytime art class because evenings are reserved for advanced students
options.D: Take the class only after she completes a beginner sketching course

questions.explanation: 男生提醒别等，两周后开放报名，晚间段很快报满。B 正确。A 与他对课程的正面态度相反，reversed_situation。C、D 没有这种内容，unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore or quiet-hours schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Can we finally settle the chores and quiet-hours thing? I think we've been winging it for three weeks.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, agreed. The dishes have piled up twice already.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was thinking we split it by day. You take dishes Monday and Wednesday, I take Tuesday and Thursday, and we trade Friday weekly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Friday I'm usually out, so weekly trade works if you start. What about the trash?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Whoever's day it isn't, takes it down. That keeps it even.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Fair. And quiet hours? I have an 8 a.m. class three days a week, so anything past 11 is hard.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Eleven works for me, but I sometimes have group projects that go late. Could I use headphones after eleven if I'm on a call?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, as long as the conversation stays at a low volume. And the desk lamp instead of the overhead, if I'm sleeping.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Done. I'll write it up and stick it on the fridge.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Make sure the trash rule is in there. That's the one we always forget.

questions.stem: What are the two roommates mainly trying to do?

options.A: Agree on a written schedule for chores and quiet hours
options.B: Decide whether to move into a larger apartment next semester
options.C: Complain to the resident advisor about a noisy neighbor
options.D: Plan a weekend study session for their group project

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕家务分工和安静时段安排，最后女生还说要写下来贴冰箱上。A 正确。B、C、D 都没有出现，都是 wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how the roommates handle late-night noise?

options.A: They are unable to compromise and need a third party to mediate
options.B: They will rely on the resident advisor to enforce a strict 11 p.m. cutoff
options.C: They agree to flexibility past 11 p.m. as long as headphones and low-volume conversation are used
options.D: They have decided to ban all late-night calls or group work entirely

questions.explanation: 男生同意女生 11 点后可以用耳机低声通话，作为安静时段的灵活安排。C 正确。A、B、D 都与对话中达成的妥协相反，reversed_situation。
